changeReason: Improved translation from Japanese. Old: "Swordsmanship 201: Advanced Course"

TextENGNew: ``...Ouka.@k@vS20/45/VTT_hanyu3141. I pray there will never be a time where you have to use those sword skills... but the world is unpredictable.``
``Therefore, I shall teach you the resolve you will require in such a situation.``
``First of all, never let your guard down, even against an unskilled opponent.``

``Unlike men, a woman's strength has its limits no matter how hard you train.@k@vS20/45/VTT_hanyu3145. So no matter how you may master your craft, you cannot escape that disadvantage if the fight comes down to brute strength.``
``Then... merely being armed with a sword will not scare my opponent all that much?``

``That is right.@k@vS20/45/VTT_hanyu3147. They will be bewildered... but the first thing they will feel afterwards is curiosity and scorn.@k@vS20/45/VTT_hanyu3148. An amateur who cannot gauge the difference in strength is almost never scared.``
``...However, please remember that there is one definitive way to win in that situation.``
``? What's that?``

``Basically, your opponent will unconsciously increase the gap between you two after the first move in an attempt to make you surrender.@k@vS20/45/VTT_hanyu3151. That is because the first move is very important in establishing psychological superiority.``
``...However, in that situation, they will be virtually defenseless.@k@vS20/45/VTT_hanyu3153. So whether you seize that chance or not greatly influences the tide of the battle.``
``So you mean I should make the most of whatever openings I can find?``

``In addition, while it is easy to hit with slashes, it is just as easy for your opponent to defend against them because they manifest as straight ``lines``.@k@vS20/45/VTT_hanyu3155. However, since a thrust manifests as a single ``point``, it is difficult to defend and avoid those.``
``...Therefore, since a woman is physically inferior, a thrust is the deadliest and most effective strike in your arsenal.``

``Incidentally, the ideal thrust is actually two motions: a feint to create an opening, and then the killing blow.@k@vS20/45/VTT_hanyu3158. However, it requires considerable skill and judgment, so it's still too early to teach it to you...``
``Also... what do you think is the easiest and most effective location to strike in a thrust?``
``Well... I would have to say the chest or the throat?``

``No.@k@vS20/45/VTT_hanyu3161. When you attack someone, they will instinctively try to protect their vitals.@k@vS20/45/VTT_hanyu3162. In that situation, the first thing they move... are their hands.@k@vS20/45/VTT_hanyu3163. And naturally, their hands are what primarily moves when they attack.``
``...In other words, the hands are the body's attack and defense organs, and are always unconsciously moving based on what you're thinking.``

``However, in exchange, people aren't very conscious about protecting their own hands.@k@vS20/45/VTT_hanyu3166. Thus, when they are attacked, their reaction when it comes to defending their hands is slower than anywhere else in the body.``
``But... I would think it'd be difficult to hit a moving target.``

``Even so, it's not easy for them to change their trajectories in the middle of an action, so it's easy to predict where they'll go.@k@vS20/45/VTT_hanyu3168. So when thrusting, first go for the hands...``
